description:
Follow-on to bl-bd85's decision record. The RustCrypto sha1+hex chain (~8 transitive crates: sha1, digest, block-buffer, crypto-common, generic-array, typenum, cpufeatures, cfg-if) is used in exactly two sites — Task::generate_id and stealth_tasks_dir — both producing persisted, location-determining values that MUST stay byte-identical to today's output.

WHY (epic bl-32f8 context): vendoring is the one remaining footprint lever that respects every constraint the bl-bd85 decision identified — preserves exact hash values (no migration, no orphaned stealth stores), works in no-git/stealth mode, removes ~8 crates. The trade is owning a tiny hand-maintained crypto primitive in the tracker. SHA-1 is frozen (FIPS 180-4), ~60 lines pure-Rust, has canonical NIST test vectors so the 100% coverage gate is satisfiable cleanly.

SCOPE: (a) new module src/hash.rs implementing sha1 + lowercase hex of the digest, under 300 lines; (b) src/hash_tests.rs with NIST FIPS 180-4 vectors covering the short-padding path, the two-block padding path, and the multi-block full-input path (1M-'a' canonical vector); (c) replace both call sites and remove sha1 + hex from Cargo.toml; (d) verify cargo tree drops the full RustCrypto chain and that bl create on a known (title, timestamp) yields the same id it did before this change. Behavior MUST be byte-identical — this is a no-migration swap. Final tree should be 39 -> ~31 crates.
title: Vendor a minimal SHA-1; drop sha1 + hex crates
